findings.evidence: Mathlib has `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Set/Finite/Basic.lean:566 theorem Finite.image` and `:571 lemma Finite.of_surjOn`; the PR proof at `code/TauCeti/FieldTheory/IntermediateField/Card.lean:47` is `(hT.image _).subset (subset_image_adjoin_simple h)`.
findings.fix: Remove this public theorem and use `Set.Finite.of_surjOn`/`hT.image` plus the local subset/surjOn proof at the bounded-root call site.
findings.issue: `finite_of_forall_exists_mem_eq_adjoin_simple` is just finiteness of a surjective image specialized to `x ↦ K⟮x⟯`, creating parallel API for existing Set finiteness lemmas.

findings.evidence: Mathlib has `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Set/Card.lean:670 theorem ncard_le_ncard` and `:826 theorem ncard_image_le`; the PR proof at `code/TauCeti/FieldTheory/IntermediateField/Card.lean:54` is exactly their transitive composition.
findings.fix: Do the two-line `Set.ncard_le_ncard`/`Set.ncard_image_le` argument locally in `SimpleGenerators.lean`, or factor a genuinely general Set lemma rather than an IntermediateField-specific wrapper.
findings.issue: `ncard_le_of_forall_exists_mem_eq_adjoin_simple` is the generic bound `S.ncard ≤ (f '' T).ncard ≤ T.ncard` with `f := fun x => K⟮x⟯`, so it is a thin parallel wrapper over Set cardinality API.

summary: The bounded-root specialization is new, but the new public intermediate-field helpers package existing Set image/cardinality API under a parallel field-specific namespace. Keep the bounded result and inline these generic Set steps instead of adding reusable TauCeti declarations for them.
